name: Mick Harris
description: '''Michael John Harris''' (born 4 October 1967) is an English musician from [[Birmingham]]. He was the drummer for [[Napalm Death]] between 1985 and 1991, and is credited for coining the term "[[grindcore]]". Harris is widely regarded as one of the founders of the blast beat for his incredibly fast "Chaos U.K" beat he uses all across the three N.D albums he plays on. After Napalm Death, Harris joined [[Painkiller (band)|Painkiller]] with [[John Zorn]] and [[Bill Laswell]]. Since the mid-1990s, Harris has worked primarily in [[electronic music|electronic]], [[ambient music|ambient]] and [[dub music]], his main projects being [[Scorn (band)|Scorn]] and [[Lull (band)|Lull]]. He has also collaborated with musicians including [[James Plotkin]] and [[Extreme Noise Terror]]. According to [[AllMusic]], Harris's "genre-spanning activities have done much to jar the minds, expectations, and record collections of audiences previously kept aggressively opposed."
